mapping.a_element: the percolation parameter tuned to its critical value
mapping.b_element: the localization length ξ decreased toward the scaling crossover
long-range correlations of the quasiperiodic localized eigenstates

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic notion of a transition controlled by a tuning parameter (percolation parameter vs. localization length ξ) and a concern with spatial correlations; the actual machinery differs. Paper A is a rigorous geometric-connectivity result proving the absence of infinite clusters at a critical point via probabilistic tools (ergodicity, positive association, decoupling), whereas paper B is a numerical study of a dynamical localization crossover in the Lyapunov spectrum of nonlinear unitary maps, so no role-for-role mapping of a shared governing process holds.
